commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c524c45fe9b81a21c65f8a4fbd6af4fad93c92c7)
commands.review_summary:
The branch updates Android Gateway/Talk frame handling and playback, adds Android/Web realtime transcript accumulation and chat-thread rendering, and changes realtime consult prompting/fallback behavior.

Reproducibility: no. full high-confidence live reproduction is attached. Source inspection confirms the current-main direct final-transcript path and the PR's forced-consult replacement, but audible playback and browser runtime behavior still need real device/browser proof.
commands.review_followup: Needs human maintainer decision on forced-consult policy plus contributor-supplied real Android/browser proof before merge.

target.title: Stabilize realtime Talk turns, playback, and consults
target.body:
## Summary

- Serialize Android Gateway websocket frame handling and queue realtime PCM playback so assistant audio and transcript events are applied in order.
- Accumulate realtime transcript chunks and split alternating user/assistant turns correctly in Android and Web.
- Move Web realtime Talk bubbles into the main chat thread so voice turns flow downward like normal chat instead of living inside the composer.
- Tighten realtime consult behavior for todo/list/file/current-context questions so Web realtime delegates to OpenClaw and avoids answering from summarized source output.

## Root Cause

Android Gateway websocket frames were dispatched on separate coroutines, so realtime transcript/audio events could race. Android transcript deltas were also treated like full snapshots, which could leave only the latest assistant fragment visible, and PCM chunks were written directly from event handling with partial-write/choppy-playback risk.

The shared realtime conversation accumulator only closed the active user turn when an assistant turn began. It did not consistently close the active assistant turn when a genuinely new user turn began, so alternating turns could collapse into prior bubbles.

Web additionally rendered realtime Talk turns inside the composer/input box with a capped mini-scroll area. Even when the accumulator had separate entries, the layout did not behave like the main chat transcript.

For the todo/list question, recent session history showed a consult run reading `TODO.md` through a broad command whose output was summarized, then answering from that lossy summary. The Web realtime path has more browser-owned provider behavior than Android gateway-relay, so the realtime model also needed stronger instructions to consult for todo/task/file/current-context questions.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Android realtime transcript/audio stability, Android/Web alternating Talk bubble separation, Web Talk bubble layout flow, and realtime consult accuracy for todo/list/file questions.

Real environment tested: Focused Android unit-test/ktlint environment and focused Web/Node Vitest environment in the realtime Talk PR worktree. Live session history from May 19, 2026 was inspected with personal identifiers redacted from chat reporting.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest`; `./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-conversation.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.talk.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle-connect.node.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles/chat/layout.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts`; targeted `pnpm format:check`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Android focused unit and ktlint checks passed. Web realtime conversation/view/lifecycle tests passed: 4 files, 51 tests. Web chat layout styles passed: 1 file, 8 tests. Talk consult/gateway tests passed: 3 files, 43 tests. `git diff --check` passed.

Observed result after fix: Android now keeps realtime events ordered, accumulates transcript chunks, closes old turns when the next speaker starts, and writes playback through a queued AudioTrack writer. Web now renders realtime Talk turns in the main chat thread, keeps alternating turns separate, auto-scrolls on realtime transcript updates, and gives realtime consults explicit instructions to use exact source entries for todo/list/file questions.

What was not tested: I did not run a live browser realtime Talk session after the consult-instruction patch. I also did not independently inspect the Telegram screenshot artifact inside this PR. Live Android behavior was user-confirmed after the Android-side changes, but no fresh hardware recording is attached.

## Verification

- PASS: `./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.voice.TalkModeManagerTest`
- PASS: `./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-conversation.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.talk.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-lifecycle-connect.node.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles/chat/layout.test.ts`
- PASS: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/talk/agent-consult-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/talk.test.ts`
- PASS: targeted `pnpm format:check`
- PASS: `git diff --check`
- NOT RUN: full Android app/device realtime Talk smoke and live browser realtime Talk smoke after the final consult-instruction patch.
